mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns exceed 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or 20d returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a lagging federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces expectations of aggressive policy tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 4.0%, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of months with positive payroll revisions, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% quarter-over-quarter indicates severe economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or signal fails to predict direction during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signals produce negative 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in logistic regression.
